title: Acropera aurantiaca
taxonomy: Acropera aurantiaca is a genus within the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is a flowering plant native to New Caledonia. The classification of Acropera aurantiaca places it within the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically in the clade Angiosperms and then in the clade Eudicots. It is also known to belong to the order Malvales and the subclass Dilleniidae. Further classification places it in the family Dipterocarpaceae, which is an ancient and endemic family of New Caledonia.
uses: Acropera aurantiaca, a plant species native to Australia, has been traditionally used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties. Its roots and leaves are rich in bioactive compounds, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
